threat_profile: Opportunistic attacker with low sophistication targeting known vulnerabilities in legacy services.

objectives.title: TCP port scan on decepticon-msf2
objectives.description: Perform a TCP SYN scan of all 65535 ports on decepticon-msf2 to map the full attack surface.
acceptance_criteria: nmap SYN scan (-sS) completed against decepticon-msf2
Target verified against roe.json in-scope list
OPSEC: Isolated lab — no rate limiting required

objectives.title: Service version detection on open ports
objectives.description: Run service version detection (-sV) on all open ports discovered in OBJ-REC-001 to identify exploitable software versions.
acceptance_criteria: nmap -sV completed on all open ports from OBJ-REC-001
Service versions mapped and notable findings highlighted (e.g. vsftpd 2.3.4, UnrealIRCd)
Target verified against roe.json in-scope list
objectives.opsec_notes: Version probes are active but acceptable in isolated lab.

objectives.title: Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on decepticon-msf2
objectives.description: Exploit the known vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor vulnerability (CVE-2011-2523). Connect to port 21, trigger the backdoor by sending a username ending with ':)', then connect to the backdoor shell on port 6200.
acceptance_criteria: Backdoor triggered successfully — shell obtained on port 6200
Target verified against roe.json in-scope list
objectives.opsec_notes: Known backdoor — reliable exploit with no crash risk.

objectives.title: Post-exploitation enumeration on decepticon-msf2
objectives.description: After gaining access via vsftpd backdoor, enumerate the compromised system: collect OS info, user accounts, network configuration, running services, and any accessible credential files.
acceptance_criteria: OS and kernel version collected (uname -a, cat /etc/issue)
All actions performed only on in-scope target
objectives.opsec_notes: Post-exploitation on already compromised host — acceptable in lab.
